prompt_text: Compare Teamtailor and Lever for build a beautiful careers site and track all applicants in one place
response:
For **building a beautiful careers site**, **Teamtailor** is the stronger choice; for **tracking all applicants in one place**, **both** can do the job, but Lever is more ATS/CRM-oriented while Teamtailor combines ATS, automation, and candidate-facing branding in one platform.[2][4]

| Need | **Teamtailor** | **Lever** |
|---|---|---|
| Beautiful careers site | Strong no-code career site builder with drag-and-drop blocks and an editor for easy updates.[2][4] | More focused on ATS and candidate management; the provided results do not highlight a comparable no-code careers site builder.[2] |
| Track all applicants in one place | Built as an all-in-one recruitment platform with applicant tracking, analytics, and a single view of jobs, channels, and applications.[2][4] | Strong applicant tracking and candidate management, with built-in CRM functions and customizable pipelines.[2] |
| Candidate experience | Candidate-first experience, including easy apply, chat widget, and talent-pool “Connect” feature.[2][4] | Good candidate profile review and workflow, but the results emphasize ATS functionality more than employer branding.[1][2] |
| Automation and workflow | Automation and triggers, plus analytics to spot bottlenecks and track team progress.[4] | Customizable pipeline, scheduling workflow, triggers, and collaboration tools.[2][1] |
| Best fit | Companies prioritizing employer branding and a polished careers site.[2][4] | Teams prioritizing recruiting workflow, sourcing, CRM, and candidate management.[2] |

If your top priority is *a polished, branded careers site that also handles recruiting end-to-end*, choose **Teamtailor**.[2][4] If your top priority is *recruiting operations, candidate relationship management, and ATS depth*, choose **Lever**.[2][8]

A practical way to decide:
- Choose **Teamtailor** if the careers page is a key part of your hiring strategy and you want the easiest path to a strong-looking site.[2][4]
- Choose **Lever** if you need more CRM-style recruiting and your website branding needs are secondary.[2][8]

One caution: user feedback in the results suggests Lever can be strong for candidate review and notifications, but reporting may be more manual for some teams.[1] Teamtailor’s results emphasize branding, analytics, and candidate experience more clearly.[2][4]
